message:
feat(viz): redesign hero as ambient data rain with perspective grid

Replace the "fountain droplet" hero animation with a calmer "data rain" aesthetic
that better matches the article's editorial tone:

Color Palette (COLORS constant):
- Adjust white from #f8fafc to #f1f5f9 and slate from #94a3b8 to #64748b to
  match the updated CSS design system from the previous commit

Three.js Hero Rewrite:
- Increase particle count from 2,000 to 3,000 for denser coverage at the new
  lower opacity (0.5→0.4) and smaller point size (0.8→0.5)
- Remove per-particle size attribute; add per-particle velocity array so each
  symbol falls at a slightly different speed (0.1–0.5), creating depth parallax
- Flatten camera angle: remove the y=15 upward tilt, push z from 50 to 60 for
  a wider, more ambient field of view
- Expand particle spread from 80×100×40 to 120×120×100 for full-viewport fill
- Simplify reset logic: particles wrap from y=-60 to y=60 instead of calling a
  full resetDroplet() function on each recycle
- Slow global rotation from 0.002 to 0.001 rad/frame
- Add a subtle THREE.GridHelper floor (200×40 subdivisions, cyan/dark, 10%
  opacity, y=-50) to ground the particle field and add spatial reference

These changes make the hero feel more like a data visualization and less like
a particle toy, setting the right tone before the reader hits the first section.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
